summary: Frontend e2e of the 3 new admin pages (Simpro, Microsoft 365, TextMagic) + Integrations tile page + worker permission gating + mask preservation. All 7 review scenarios PASSED end-to-end against the live preview backend. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: IntegrationFormUI.jsx is a clean shared component module (AdminCard / StatusPill / Field / Input / InputWithToggle / SavedChip / authErrorToast). authErrorToast correctly suppresses 401 (handled by axios interceptor) and surfaces a friendly 403 message — exactly what the worker-gating test exercised.

Simpro/Microsoft365/TextMagic admin pages all follow identical dual-state pattern (secretInput vs secretOnFile, isMasked echo, ••••xxxx Saved chip, Editing chip while typing). Backend mask-preserve on PUT verified by reload-and-partial-save test on TextMagic — api_key on file stayed ••••1234 after a username-only save.

Integrations.jsx pulls live /api/integrations status and merges it into the existing INTEGRATIONS mock metadata via KIND_MAP. All 4 tiles route to the correct admin routes and render the correct status pill colour mapping (connected/error/not_connected).
Microsoft365Admin gates the Connect-to-Microsoft button on setupReady=(tenant_id && client_id && (secretInput || secretOnFile)) — verified disabled before save, enabled after save.

TextMagic Test Connection correctly surfaces wrapped upstream 401 (real TextMagic returned HTTP 401 'Invalid credentials or this token has been revoked.') as a red toast + inline test-msg — no kickout, because the X-Auth-Reason header is not set on upstream 401s (correct behaviour per Phase 5 docs).
seed_data_creation: Saved dummy credentials on the org's Simpro / M365 / TextMagic integration_configs rows: Simpro client_secret=••••abcd, M365 client_secret=••••3xyz + tenant/client/sender, TextMagic api_key=••••1234 + username=TEST_user_x2. These are intentional dummy values for future regression — they will fail real upstream auth (which is exactly what the test asserted for TextMagic).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 integrations (Navixy, Simpro, M365, TextMagic) now have parity admin pages sharing IntegrationFormUI.jsx. The canonical regression flow is: admin login → /app/settings/integrations → 4 tiles render with live status → click Configure on each → save dummy creds → verify ••••xxxx Saved chip → click Test Connection → expect a real upstream-error toast (Simpro=400 'No access token', TextMagic=400 'HTTP 401 invalid creds', M365 needs a real Azure app so skipped). For mask preservation, save api_key once then change ONLY the username on a subsequent save — reload should still show ••••<last4>. Worker gating: worker can GET an admin page but PUT/POST gets 403, surfaced as a red 'You don't have permission…' toast.

evidence.integrations_page: all 4 cards render with status 'Not connected' (matches /api/integrations response)
evidence.simpro_test_msg: No access token — click Get Token first.
evidence.tm_test_msg: TextMagic auth failed: HTTP 401 {"code":401,"message":"Invalid credentials or this token has been revoked."}
evidence.worker_403_toast: You don't have permission to edit integrations. Ask an admin to enable integrations.edit for your user, or run this as an admin.
